title: Basic for Loop
instruction: Go has one loop keyword: `for`. The classic form is `for init; condition; post { }`. Write a loop that prints the numbers 1 through 5, each on its own line.

title: While-Style Loop
instruction: Omitting init and post makes `for` act like a while loop. Count down from 3 to 1, printing each number, then print "Liftoff!" after the loop.
hint: for n > 0 { fmt.Println(n); n-- } then fmt.Println("Liftoff!")

title: Range over Slice
instruction: `for range` iterates over a slice (index and value). Loop over the fruits slice and print each fruit name on its own line.

title: break and continue
instruction: `break` exits the loop; `continue` skips to the next iteration. Loop from 1 to 6: skip evens with `continue`, stop if number exceeds 5 with `break`. Print only 1, 3, and 5.
hint: if i%2 == 0 { continue }; if i > 5 { break }; fmt.Println(i)

title: Sum with a Loop
instruction: Accumulate a running total. Sum all integers from 1 to 10 and print the result (should be 55).
hint: for i := 1; i <= 10; i++ { sum += i }
